question:
Q.1 Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words.

A large number of fish swimming together
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 🐟 Collective Nouns for animals:

• **Shoal**: A large number of fish swimming together (ಮೀನುಗಳ ಗುಂಪು) - ✅.
• **Sheaf**: A bundle of grain stalks or papers.
• **String**: A group of horses.
• **Stream**: A continuous flow of liquid or people.

question:
Q.2 Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.

**Intriguing**
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 🤔 **Intriguing** means arousing one's curiosity or interest; fascinating (ಕುತೂಹಲಕಾರಿಯಾದ).

• **Interesting**: Holding the attention; arousing curiosity (ಆಸಕ್ತಿದಾಯಕ) - ✅ Synonym.
• **Ravishing/Stunning**: Extremely beautiful.
• **Glorifying**: Praising highly.

question:
Q.3 Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined words in the given sentence.

The traffic sergeant proved through the test that Raj was **under the influence of drugs and alcohol**.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 🥴 Medical/Legal Terms:

• **Inebriated**: Drunk or intoxicated by alcohol or drugs (ಮತ್ತೇರಿದ/ಕುಡಿದ ಅಮಲಿನಲ್ಲಿರುವ) - ✅.
• **Simonised**: Polished (especially a car).
• **Pocked**: Marked by pustules or pits.

question:
Q.4 Select the word with the correct spelling from the options given below to fill in the blank.

They saw the beautiful _______ of the Gods in the Temples.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: ✍️ Spelling Check:

• **Idols**: An image or representation of a god used as an object of worship (ವಿಗ್ರಹಗಳು) - ✅.
• **Idles**: Spending time doing nothing.
• **Ideals**: Standards of perfection.

question:
Q.5 Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word.

The company was engaged in **spurious** trade practices.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 🚫 **Spurious** means not being what it purports to be; false or fake (ನಕಲಿ/ಸುಳ್ಳು).

• **Fraudulent**: Obtained by or involving deception (ವಂಚನೆಯ/ಮೋಸದ) - ✅ Synonym.
• **Spacious**: Having ample space.

question:
Q.6 Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice.

She is building a new house.
options: She had built a new house.
A new house is being built by her.
She has built a new house.
A new house has been built by her.
answer: A new house is being built by her.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 📢 Present Continuous Passive:

• **Active**: Subject + is/am/are + V-ing + Object.
• **Passive**: Object + is/am/are + **being** + V3 + by Subject.
• 'Is building' becomes **'is being built'**. ✅

question:
Q.7 Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks.

As cattle are to herd so are birds to __________.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 🐦 Collective Nouns:

• **Flock**: A number of birds of one kind feeding or travelling together (ಪಕ್ಷಿಗಳ ಹಿಂಡು) - ✅.
• **Pack**: Wolves/Dogs.
• **Litter**: Kittens/Puppies.

question:
Q.8 Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence.

I always enjoy the company of **respectful** teachers.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 🙇‍♂️ **Respectful** means feeling or showing deference and respect.

• **Admiring**: Characterized by or expressive of admiration or respect (ಗೌರವಿಸುವ/ಮೆಚ್ಚುವ) - ✅ Synonym.
• **Insolent**: Rude/Disrespectful.
• **Derogatory**: Showing a critical or disrespectful attitude.

question:
Q.9 Select the most appropriate synonym of the word given in brackets to fill in the blank.

He became __________(**verbose**) after a few drinks.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 🗣️ **Verbose** means using or expressed in more words than are needed (ಅತುವಾಗಿ ಮಾತನಾಡುವ).

• **Talkative**: Fond of or given to talking (ವಾಚಾಳಿ/ಮಾತಿನ ಮಲ್ಲ) - ✅ Synonym.

question:
Q.10 Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.

**Eloquent**
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 🎤 **Eloquent** means fluent or persuasive in speaking or writing (ವಾಗ್ಮಿ/ಚತುರ).

• **Impotent**: Lacking in power or ability (ಅಸಮರ್ಥ/ಶಕ್ತಿಯಿಲ್ಲದ) - ✅ Antonym (in the context of expressive power).
• **Humble**: Low estimate of one's importance.

question:
Q.11 Select the most appropriate idiom or phrase to fill in the blank.

Sweety has been _________ since her painting won first prize.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: ☁️ **Walking on air**: To feel extremely happy or excited (ಅತೀವ ಸಂತೋಷದಲ್ಲಿರುವುದು) - ✅.
• **Walking on eggshells**: Being extremely cautious.
• **Waiting in the wings**: Ready to take over a role.

question:
Q.12 Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.

**Bad iron**
explanation: Mastery Insight: 🍀 **Bad iron** is a slang/idiomatic expression for **bad luck** or an unfortunate situation (ದುರದೃಷ್ಟ) - ✅.

question:
Q.13 Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment.

National development is defined as an improvement in **people's daily arrangements**, the provision of fundamental necessities...
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 🏠 Development Context:

• **Living conditions**: The circumstances in which people live (ಜೀವನ ಮಟ್ಟ/ಪರಿಸ್ಥಿತಿ) - ✅.
• 'Daily arrangements' is a vague phrase; 'living conditions' is the professional standard.

question:
Q.14 Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined word.

Last week's rain **ravaged** the whole city.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: ⛈️ **Ravaged** means severely damaged or devastated (ಧ್ವಂಸ ಮಾಡು).

• **Ruined**: Reduced to a state of decay or collapse (ಹಾಳುಮಾಡು) - ✅ Synonym.
• **Rescinded**: Cancelled a law or order.

question: Q.15 Select the one-word substitute for: **A sentimental memory of the past**
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 🎞️ Emotions:

• **Nostalgia**: A sentimental longing or wistful affection for the past (ಹಳೆಯ ನೆನಪುಗಳ ಮೆಲುಕು) - ✅.
• **Euphoria**: Extreme happiness.
• **Mania**: Obsessive enthusiasm.

question:
Q.16 Identify the segment that contains an error.

**Sajni had / interfere in / this matter.**
explanation:
Mastery Insight: ❌ Past Perfect Tense Error:

• After **'had'**, the past participle (**V3**) must be used.
• **Correction**: 'Sajni had **interfered** in this matter'. ✅

question: Q.17 Select the sentence that contains no spelling errors.
options: The mall road of our city is always tiiming with street vendors.
The mall road of our city is always teeming with street vendors.
The mall road of our city is always tyming with street vendors.
The mall road of our city is always teyming with street vendors.
answer: The mall road of our city is always teeming with street vendors.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: ✍️ Spelling Check:

• **Teeming**: To be full of or swarming with (ತುಂಬಿ ತುಳುಕುತ್ತಿರುವುದು) - ✅.
• Correct spelling is T-E-E-M-I-N-G.

question:
Q.18 Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice.

He does not buy shirts.
options: Shirts were not been bought by him.
Shirts are not brought by him.
Shirts were not bought by him.
Shirts are not bought by him.
answer: Shirts are not bought by him.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 📢 Simple Present Passive:

• **Active**: Subject + do/does + not + V1 + Object.
• **Passive**: Object + is/am/are + not + V3 + by Subject.
• 'Does not buy' becomes **'are not bought'** (since 'shirts' is plural). ✅

question:
Q.19 Arrange the jumbled sentences (P, Q, R, S) between S1 and S6 to form a meaningful paragraph.

(S1) Today, the world has become a much smaller place, thanks to the miracles of science.
(P) We are slowly realising that the world is a single cooperative group.
(Q) Other religions have become forces with which we have to reckon...
(R) Mingling of population is bringing about interchange of thought.
(S) Foreign nations have become our next-door neighbours.
(S6) We cannot have religious unity and peace so long as we assert that we are in possession of the light...
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 🧩 Logical Flow:

• S1 mentions the world becoming smaller.
• **S**: Defines 'smaller' by saying nations are neighbours.
• **R**: Explains the result of being neighbours (mingling/interchange).
• **P**: Shows the collective realization (cooperative group).
• **Q**: Transitions to the religious theme mentioned in S6. ✅

question: Q.20 Identify the sentence that correctly uses the indefinite article.
options: She purchased apple in the market with me.
She purchase a apple in the market with me.
She purchased a apple in the market with me.
She purchased an apple in the market with me.
answer: She purchased an apple in the market with me.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 🅰️ Article Rules:

• 'Apple' starts with a vowel sound ('A').
• Rule: Use **'an'** before words starting with vowel sounds. ✅

question: Cloze Test (1/5): All state universities in Uttar Pradesh, one of the (1) _______ in India, will now...
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 📏 Superlative Degrees:

• Structure: 'one of the + superlative adjective + plural noun'.
• **Correction**: 'one of the **biggest states**' (ದೊಡ್ಡ ರಾಜ್ಯಗಳಲ್ಲಿ ಒಂದು). ✅

question: Cloze Test (2/5): ...will now (2) _______ a “save water” ritual named ‘Jal Bharo’...
explanation:
Mastery Insight: ⏳ Modal Verbs:

• The modal verb **'will'** is always followed by the base form of the verb (**V1**).
• **Correction**: will **organise** (ಆಯೋಜಿಸುವುದು). ✅

question: Cloze Test (3/5): ‘Jal Bharo’ signifies (3) _______ the vision of ‘Jal Shakti’.
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 💪 Context:

• A ritual like 'Jal Bharo' aims to make a vision more powerful or effective.
• **Strengthening**: Making something stronger (ಬಲಪಡಿಸುವುದು) - ✅. ✅

question: Cloze Test (4/5): This ceremony symbolises the (4) _______ importance of water conservation...
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 🌊 Degree of Importance:

• **Immense**: Extremely large or great (ಅಪಾರ/ಅತೀವ) - ✅.
• Used to emphasize the high priority of water conservation.

question: Cloze Test (5/5): It is really a (5) _______ initiative. Every educational institute in India should ensure...
explanation:
Mastery Insight: 👏 Positive Adjectives:

• **Laudable**: Deserving praise and commendation (ಶ್ಲಾಘನೀಯ) - ✅.
• The initiative is being praised as a good example for others.
